title: Exact scheduling and optimality certificates
prompt: A branch-and-bound algorithm can certify optimality for a deterministic parallel-machine scheduling model, given enough time. Under short time limits it may return a feasible incumbent and a bound. Explain the managerial distinction between a good schedule and a certificate without promising certification by every deadline.

model_specification.mechanism: Bounding proves no better schedule exists when the incumbent and bound meet; otherwise their gap quantifies unresolved optimality.

fidelity_requirements: Certificate is conditional on closing the gap
prohibited_inferences: Every returned schedule is proven optimal
